item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: agreed. I would actually suggest that it takes more strength to know when not to fight than it does to just blindly charge into any battle, esp when that's the stuff that gets yr blood up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: RockTeethMothEyes why did you send me here now i'm crying +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a forward: I only skimmed the discussion before writing this because I have a lot to say and while I don't want to say anything already said,I forget my own thoughts easily.

This art (and I consider all entertainment a kind of art), while.. eaten, let's say, as part of an SCP style buffet is fine. Feels a bit too.. Hamburger with fries when compared to things like Red Reality (a 10 course meal that you cant stop until you finish, feeling full but sick) or Hateful Star (a simple meal that requires eating various sides and trying 4 different wines to fully appriciate).

But, either on it's own as a fun read or if it were stripped of SCPs world building and given it's own solid foundation…
This could make a fine novel or a perfectly enjoyable film.
The narrative is told through found footage, and that's acceptable. THE MAZE 2 was basically a script as well and it worked, just as this does.

I liked it. It pulled me in, and by the end, Chris Hemsworth was voicing Androcles in my mind and I had a great desire to write a story about MTF Chain Gang.

I understand the no votes and the distaste for it, but I hope that the author reads this and knows that it touched me. It may have felt out of place when compared to others, but I found it to be a hidden treat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WOW. I'd upvote twice if I could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cliches aren't necessarily a bad thing. If they didn't work, they never would have become cliches. Even the most predictable, overused trope can be great in the right context…

But I'd say this is excessive. The containment procedures and the description make this scip seem like an potentially decent subversion of the Able-type living weapon archetype, with the weird tumour thing, involuntary weapon creation, and general dislike of combat making him seem like he's not quite the living weapon you'd expect him to be.

Unfortunately, that's only the beginning of the article. Most of the article is in the attachments, and those feel like a cliche after cliche. The plot is fairly predictable and there are so many overused tropes. The dialogue is mostly decent, but nothing to write home about. Overall, it feels unoriginal, like a story I've read many times before.

With that said, it did somehow still draw me in. I don't know what it is about it that kept me reading, but once I started, I didn't stop until I was done. Perhaps it was because of the minor questions I still wanted answered, like the exact mechanics of X06. Or maybe it was simply because I found it easy to read.

The story is well paced, well structured, and flows really well. There are no jarring changes of setting or topic, no minor details given excessive attention, no random tangents that suddenly snapping back into the previous topic. It just works.

I'm half tempted to downvote because of the predictable plot and numerous cliches, but it has just enough redeeming qualities to keep me from doing so.

No vote.

item.title: Re: Dunno.
item.content:encoded: I will agree it is lengthier but I felt that Androcles needed to begin and end in one single story. There is no place where I felt Like I could split it that would have been adequate.

item.title: Dunno.
item.content:encoded: This is long and very story. I don't quite understand why it needs to be a skip. Why not split it to a skip and a tale? No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don’t usually read the long Skips. But this? This was well worth the length.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. This was… amazing. It took me through one hell of an emotional rollercoaster and was certainly not what I was expecting when I first started reading (but a surprise I was in no way upset to receive, of course).

I especially liked the kind of scary it was. Like, it was scary, but not because you were scared to look behind you for fear that you might be devoured by some horrible abomination; you were scared for the characters. Even the minor, unnamed ones were imbued with thought, backstories, and personality—they were all characters you could root for, and that leaves an impression on me, personally, more than most anything else. Except for the antagonists, of course, but they're admirable because you portrayed their innate badness and evil so well. They are not admirable as people, but they are admirable as characters because you managed to perfect them and their motives so well.

Anyway, this is great. Sorry if my ramblings were like, not helpful :'D I can most certainly appreciate good writing, but I am not a writer myself, so I can't really look at it from an objective standpoint. My feedback can only be based on the feelings I was left with after reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After reading all this, all I can say is…

MTF Chain Gang all the way!

Seriously though, seeing all these people work together to fight a common enemy was so awesome. Absolutely loved this!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks, you made me feel what sadness was again. A great read.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure if id say hes a pussy, he just doesent want to fight even if hes very good at it, that doesent make him a pussy for avoiding conflict

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
> Captain Holly Shore

/me squints

Artemis Fowl reference?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think I've enjoyed giving something a +1 as much as I have for this piece.

Your ability to craft with words is astounding, and I stand in awe.

Thank you for sharing such a beautiful piece of your soul with us.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely ADORE this article. Only a couple of critiques/suggestions that are largely just personal preference:

1. Androcles' mental state deteriorating is implied throughout, but only shown directly in a few places. The fear that the MTFs show toward Androcles would be more understandable if his "old self" began to surface more than it did prior to the containment breach.

2. Would you be open to adding a Ethics Committee log at the end of the article that contains the events of Hargraves' hearing? (For an example, look at the end of SCP-2700-EX's article). imo it would be more effective to SHOW Hargraves getting his ass judicially handed to him instead of simply having Fitz-Patrick TELL us he's going to.

item.title: Crybaby Me
item.content:encoded:
Welp, I'm in tears. Congrats. I cannot express how well this was written. Please, do whatever you must. I want to see this further. It's a goddamn movie just waiting to happen I tell ya!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yeah, there's a lot of Reach references in here, like the MTF's ranks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Potentially dumb question, but were you excited about Halo Reach while writing this? I found two sections so far that seem to be copying Reach quotes, plus the dude's a spartan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Lots of (too much) cliche. The Mad Doctor over his head thinks he can play god, everyone's pissed at him, "No I won't kill you, you don't deserve that" is super cringy, X06 is just misunderstood!, the Big Bad reveals himself at the end, the dramatics and superheroing. Theres a lot about the plot thats just whacky.

When I saw all the body cameras logs, and thought McCalloway died on the first one, I fully expected Zimmerman to die next but laughed when he simply accidentally turned his off.

Still, I like this. I normally wouldn't rate such a meh anomaly that basically boils down to Human+ with a +1, but you wanted to write a story, and you wrote it so interestingly enough with a few laughs here and there and an interesting plot that feels like its the script to an actual short movie that it succeeded well enough to keep me glued to my seat. The D Class part was genius and I want MTF Chain Gang to photobomb every other article on the wiki, crank their hogs and fuck. shit. up.

Well earned +1.

item.title: AUGH FUCK
item.content:encoded:
WHY OP? RIGHT IN MY FEELS!
Good work tho.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don’t have any real critique; I just want to saw that I really enjoyed this piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm just a reader with no critique to offer but i really enjoyed this so thanks for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright this is such an abysmal take.

  1. He has two friends, 3.5 by the end of the story. The MTF members all are scared of him. His only friends are Holly and Eleanor because they've interacted with each other for two years. Zimmerman only likes him because Androcles saved his life, even though he had every reason to think he was dead.
  2. Argos is his friend because he was made entirely out of Andy's blood and there's an implied psychic connection.
  3. Hargraves is an opportunistic fuck who only treats Androcles well because he wants to use him for his own personal gain. It's the same amount of respect as a worker and a tool.
  4. Yeah? It's a story about him? I do not see the complaint here.
  5. He's not a "guy that fights good." He's a man struggling with the morality of a world completely alien to his own. How does he know what's right? Can he find something worth fighting for? Is the concept of objective good even real?
  6. Yeah, it references Able. But the similarities aren't as immense as you imply. The idea of a perfect soldier or an immortal warrior permeate fiction and Able by no means holds all the cards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm surprised that nobody mentioned how close Androcles is to a textbook "Gary Stu". He is loved by everyone he met, even an X-06, his "enemy" that is the director respect him — as far as fighting capacity is concerned, and the plot all revolves around him. There is no event in the article that is not because of him. He is a guy that fights good. Even the characters mentioned that he is Able but "good", and that kind mention felt too on-the-nose to be cheeky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a little cliche and Androcles is a little bit of a pussy, but Hargreaves being brought to justice earns a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aye, fair enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. I'll also mention that in a piece of this length, you really want more images to help keep the reader engaged, direct the mood, and break up the visual profile. It's pretty easy to let things glaze over when you another identical massive blockquote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I took a look at this yesterday when it was in the drafting phase, but didn’t get to far. I really liked the concept, all I have to do now is finish reading it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. The more you know. But in this instance, there is DNA present in the gold filaments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
DNA within these cells is only 95% similar to humans and composes approximately 32.3% of the red blood cells present in SCP-4793's body.

Red blood cells don't have DNA.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your crit means so much to me and I’m working on a fix for that.

Update. 15/11/19 - 5 EST. Went through and edited almost every line of dialogue on my phone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconded FWIW.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright, well, there's certainly a lot here, which is definitely something. It's definitely a lot to get through.

My problem with this piece is the dialogue. I don't really know how to expand on this, so suffice to say that I don't really buy it. The things they're saying don't really meet the bar, I feel.

Here's one example that I think is sort of indicative of the entire piece, and while it's probably not the most problematic few lines in the article, it's definitely not super.

Zimmerman: I don't. Toss and turn every night. See a lot of faces, people I've killed, [Cough.] people who've died. But it's not as tough when you realize you're suffering [Cough.] so billions of others can sleep without thinking about anything but their boring lives.

[Silence.]

Zimmerman: We die in the dark, so they can live in the light.

Especially that last line… it feels like a script someone in the fan community might write for an SCP movie. There is no part of me that believes for even a moment that real people would ever talk like that.

There are a lot of examples of this, but honestly it's just too long to go through and name them all. I can tell you put a lot of work into this and I admire the effort, but too much of this article relies on the interactions between these characters to just ignore this. It's so hard to get through sometimes because I'm being pulled out of the article so frequently.

I don't know what I'd recommend to fix that issue - all I can say is that in its current form, for me, it's not really there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's about time! Here's to the excellent SCP we've all been waiting for!

item.title: From Stone To Flesh
item.content:encoded:
I meant to write the author post, like a month ago. But here it is.

When I started writing this, it was intended to be a joke. A goofy, pacifist character who detonated a nuke while yelling, "Behold the power of my Stand!" As you can tell, almost nothing of that idea remains. Instead, it became both a hate letter and a lamentation for SCP-076. As you can probably tell, I really dislike Able. It's a waste of a cool concept. An immortal, regenerating warrior who can make weapons. But because he stands for nothing, fights for nothing, believes in nothing, he is nothing. I tried to follow those same ideas and make something better out of that.

Enter my bizarre mishmash of Greek mythos and imagery. When I started writing Androcles as a character, I wanted to take that beautiful Greek Hero Archetype and twist it with a dash of realism. If you spend your life fighting, even for good causes, it will invariably affect you. Now that he finds himself out of time, he has a fresh start. The main character is a broken man looking to fix himself, to heal his wounds. But Hargraves is the Hera to his Hercules, forcing him from a life of peace until he becomes Perseus, winding his way through the labyrinth to fight his Minotaur. To fight the X06s, which represent the crueler and more war-like parts of himself.

As you can probably guess, Hargraves draws on Icarus. He flew too close to the sun. Simple. While Fitz-Patrick and Shore served as Athena, Holly was also representative of Aphrodite, being his closest friend and love interest.

Spoilers
Close

To circle around to the idea I presented in the beginning, that conflict for moral reasons still damages you, I put Androcles against the X06-3, who as I said before is the personification of his own more warlike and cruel spirit. While the arc about Androcles' morals came to a close with his sparing of Hargraves, this isn't where his identity arc ends. I tried to hint at it through the emails, specifically the duel, or when he called himself "an immortal being," but Androcles is a version of Ares. (Think Dog and God from Fallout New Vegas: Dead Money)

Behind The Myth:

The Greek Gods are defined by their humanity. They make mistakes. They question themselves. They change throughout the tales and myths they occupy. This is reflective of both mankind and the tradition of verbal storytelling. In my story, I decide to play Ares as a warlike being who represents war but does not control it. While the first email translation is very loose, it's meant to be interpreted as Ares struggling with his own ideas of war and morality. Who else but the God of War could have battled the God of War for ten straight days?

During this, Ares emerged victorious until he met Arabel, an avatar of a Celtic goddess, who was able to bring Androcles to the surface. Together, they planned to bring peace to the world, which stood in contrast to what Ares wanted. During a battle against another Deity of War, Ares emerged and killed both Arabel and the other deity. Traumatized by what he had done and the fact he was unable to control himself, he sealed himself away.

The End:

During the breach, especially the final fight, he comes to terms with who he is and what he has done.

There are three ways to think about what happened next, all of which are valid.

  1. He removed Ares from himself.
  2. He lost himself to Ares.
  3. He reconciled both sides of himself to become greater than the sum of his parts.

If you like one of these, that's great. If you like the idea that he's a human capable of great things, that's also great. Both views are equally valid and since I will not likely return to write more about Androcles, it doesn't really change anything.

This 16,000-word monstrosity wouldn't be possible with help, so here's a shortlist of everyone who read this at some point. There's more, I just have to find my notebook <3

Calibri Bold
IronDruid
Kybard
cybersqyd
MirageTD
Navimaster
Simartar
gee0765
DrAkimoto
TheMightyMcB
